response.justification: Mohammed Al-Said is a broadly experienced web and full-stack developer with over 15 years of professional history, but is a poor fit for this role on multiple critical dimensions. Most disqualifying is the complete absence of Japanese language proficiency, which is listed as a hard requirement. Beyond that, the candidate's background is overwhelmingly in web, mobile, and cloud-based systems (TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB) with only a brief 6-month stint writing C/C++ at National Instruments. There is no demonstrated experience in embedded networking, switch firmware, Layer-2/3 security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X), Linux kernel modification, or any of the core security technologies the role demands.
strengths: 15+ years of diverse software development experience spanning multiple languages, platforms, and domains, demonstrating strong adaptability
Some exposure to C/C++ and Linux at National Instruments and in personal projects (Sound Position Server using C++ and Linux/JACK)
Demonstrated experience with low-latency, high-throughput systems at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic, showing comfort with performance-sensitive engineering
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — a stated hard requirement that alone disqualifies the candidate
No experience with embedded networking security (MACsec, IEEE 802.1X, Captive Portal, device tamper detection, L2/L3 switching, NETCONF, SNMP)
C programming experience is minimal and dated (one 6-month role in 2014–2015); the candidate's core expertise is TypeScript/Node.js/web stack
